skill.name: designing-frontend
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents to create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction, accessibility checks, and context-specific design decisions.
skill.summary: Create distinctive frontend interfaces with strong aesthetics and practical implementation checks.
skill.author: Byunk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in a frontend design guidance document. SKILL.md contains no scripts, commands, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from broad design instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static findings at SKILL.md lines 2, 3, 6, 57, 58, and 60 appear to match substrings in ordinary design text, not cryptographic APIs or algorithms. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, weak ciphers, or security-sensitive cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose about frontend design and implementation principles. There is no code or instruction that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static findings at SKILL.md lines 33, 39, 47, and 59 are design recommendations about fonts, color combinations, layout conventions, and repeated choices. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment inspection, filesystem probing, or command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visual design guidance only. They do not ask the model to inspect system state or collect local information.

content.value_statement: Many AI-generated interfaces look generic and ignore visual context. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward distinctive, production-ready frontend design.
actual_capabilities: Defines a workflow for concept, implementation, verification, and iteration.
Guides selection of typography, color, motion, spacing, and visual effects.
Encourages context-specific design choices instead of repeated generic layouts.
Supports implementation in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend stacks.
Includes verification prompts for visual hierarchy, cohesion, interactions, animations, and accessibility.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include reusable components or code templates.
It does not define detailed accessibility acceptance criteria or automated tests.
It may encourage bold visual choices that need product or brand review.
It does not provide backend, deployment, analytics, or data integration guidance.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Memorable Product Screen
use_cases.description: Create a frontend direction that feels specific to the product, audience, and workflow.

use_cases.title: Build a Polished Web Component
use_cases.description: Ask an AI coding agent to implement a component with strong hierarchy, motion, and responsive behavior.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Generic AI Interface
use_cases.description: Turn a plain generated page into a more distinctive design while preserving usability.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the designing-frontend skill to create a single responsive landing page for [product]. Choose one clear aesthetic direction and implement it with accessible HTML and CSS.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the designing-frontend skill to improve this [component or page]. Keep the current functionality, strengthen hierarchy, refine spacing, and add context-specific visual details.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-State Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a production workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the designing-frontend skill to design and implement [workflow]. Include loading, empty, error, and success states with cohesive typography, color, motion, and accessibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Define and Execute an Art Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a distinctive product surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the designing-frontend skill to define a bold art direction for [audience and product]. Then implement the first screen and verify hierarchy, interaction quality, animation, and responsive behavior.

output_examples.input: Design a dashboard for a boutique analytics tool.
output_examples.output: A refined dashboard direction with expressive typography, restrained data density, sharp accent colors, and clear checks for hierarchy and accessibility.

output_examples.input: Make this generated pricing page feel less generic.
output: A stronger visual concept tied to the product category.
Reworked spacing, type scale, color accents, and hover states.
A verification pass for responsiveness and readable contrast.

output_examples.input: Create a product detail page for a premium hardware device.
output_examples.output: A context-specific product page plan and implementation focused on material, depth, motion, and a first-screen visual signature.

best_practices: Give the agent the product purpose, audience, brand constraints, and frontend stack before asking for implementation.
Ask for verification of accessibility, responsive layout, interactions, and visual hierarchy after the first build.
Use the skill for visual direction and implementation details, then review the result against real user needs.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to override an established design system without stakeholder approval.
Do not request decorative complexity when the workflow requires speed, clarity, or dense repeated use.
Do not treat visual distinctiveness as a replacement for accessibility, performance, or usability testing.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding agents create distinctive frontend pages, components, and applications with stronger visual direction.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not include scripts, commands, or packaged components.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this useful for production work?
faq.answer: Yes, but teams should still run normal code review, accessibility checks, and browser testing.

faq.question: Can it work with React or Vue?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly references HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend implementation paths.

faq.question: What is the main limitation?
faq.answer: It provides design and implementation guidance, not ready-made components, automated tests, or brand-specific rules.
